acceptance_targets: [chapter_16_award_01] Pass if the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:367 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:411 still surface at least two distinct motives, but no single June speech cleanly synthesizes them into a finished explanation; at least one ugly motive must emerge through interruption, evasion, or a concrete anecdote that Lena has to press or name. Fail if June still gives back-to-back polished summaries that explain her resentment for the reader.

[chapter_16_prose_01] Pass if, across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:415, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:481, no more than two still rely on explicit contrastive-negation setup and the rest make their distinction through concrete detail. Fail if three or more of those moments still use the same negation template.

[chapter_16_prose_02] Pass if, in the revised versions of the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:301, no more than two clauses use the room as acting subject or emotional proxy, and at least three of those beats instead pin pressure to Lena's body, a specific object, or shared neighborhood space. Fail if the revision still keeps handing the emotional turn to what 'the room' does.

[FA-002] Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7526 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7766 use the same corrected amounts and wire date as the revised chapter-15 evidence packet and the earlier transfer scenes. Fail if either line still says three eighty-two, six forty, two nine forty, October tenth, or any other set that does not match the book's established record.

[FA-020] Pass if both cited lines now stage June's two different modes without unreadable, blank, or flat phrasing and a reader can tell the emotional shift between them. Fail if either line still uses the stock label or if both moments remain interchangeable.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:367 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:411 still surface at least two distinct motives, but no single June speech cleanly synthesizes them into a finished explanation; at least one ugly motive must emerge through interruption, evasion, or a concrete anecdote that Lena has to press or name. Fail if June still gives back-to-back polished summaries that explain her resentment for the reader.
findings.problem: June's motive admissions are thematically correct but occasionally arrive as fully packaged theses about resentment, authorship, and class grievance, which makes the confrontation feel slightly pre-interpreted instead of maximally dangerous in the moment. That lowers the scene from shortlist-level unpredictability into tidy confession language the style bible explicitly warns against.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework June's explanatory speeches at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:275, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:295, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:367, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:411 so jealousy, opportunism, and the thrill of precision are still present but arrive through resisted admissions, concrete remembered incidents, or contradictions Lena has to force into the open, rather than June delivering a near-complete theory of herself.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:415, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:481, no more than two still rely on explicit contrastive-negation setup and the rest make their distinction through concrete detail. Fail if three or more of those moments still use the same negation template.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same contrastive-negation sentence engine: a thought is introduced by saying what it is not before the real claim lands. After enough repetitions, the rhythm stops feeling sharp and starts feeling preloaded.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the contrastive passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:415, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:481 so the distinctions are carried by image, action, or social consequence instead of repeated 'it was not/this was not' framing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised versions of the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:301, no more than two clauses use the room as acting subject or emotional proxy, and at least three of those beats instead pin pressure to Lena's body, a specific object, or shared neighborhood space. Fail if the revision still keeps handing the emotional turn to what 'the room' does.
findings.problem: Spatial pressure is one of the novel's strengths, but this chapter overuses the room itself as the acting emotional proxy. By the fourth and fifth pass, the room is repeatedly what gets smaller, holds the beat, or contains the life, and that repetition starts flattening the prose rather than intensifying it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the room-as-barometer beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:301 by relocating several of those pressure cues into Lena's body, one charged object, or the outside block geography so the scene's emotional escalation changes shape instead of repeating the same spatial shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7526 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7766 use the same corrected amounts and wire date as the revised chapter-15 evidence packet and the earlier transfer scenes. Fail if either line still says three eighty-two, six forty, two nine forty, October tenth, or any other set that does not match the book's established record.
findings.problem: The confrontation chapter repeats yet another incompatible set of transfer amounts and dates: June says three eighty-two, six forty, and two nine forty on October tenth, then Lena repeats the same numbers later. That turns the emotional reckoning into an accounting contradiction rather than a sharpening of truth.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise June's spoken recap at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7526 and Lena's demand at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7766 so both lines match the corrected record in chapter 15 and the original transfers at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2647, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3627, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4288. Keep June's blunt cadence, but have her speak the canonical amounts and the same wire date used in chapter 15 instead of inventing a third version.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines now stage June's two different modes without unreadable, blank, or flat phrasing and a reader can tell the emotional shift between them. Fail if either line still uses the stock label or if both moments remain interchangeable.
findings.problem: The novel's 9-chapter unreadable-face pattern returns twice during the confrontation itself, at lines 7440 and 7494. Using the same old mask twice at the emotional peak flattens the scene's internal turns.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7440 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7494, replace both unreadability labels with distinct confrontation behaviors: one for June's defensive control, one for the moment she starts taking inventory. The scene is too climactic to rely on the same mask twice.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
